RULING:
DEFENDANT FLOWSERVE CORPORATION SII TO DURAMETALLIC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Flowserve Corporation, successor-in-interest to Durametallic Corporation, incorrectly named as Durametallic Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 3 (false representation), 4 (false representation/fraud/concert of action) and 6 (punitive damages) and denied as to issues 1 (negligence), 2 (strict liability) and 5 (loss of consortium). Plaintiffs presented sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether Mr. Elliott was exposed to asbestos-containing packing attributable to defendant. The objections to Mr. Elliott's declaration are overruled. The court did not consider the evidence submitted with the reply papers. Plaintiffs concede the merit of issues 3, 4 and 6 as framed by defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Peter Tuann, Esq. of HOWARD ROME MARTIN & RIDLEY LLP (650-365-7715), appearing for defendant / moving party IMO Industries, Inc.
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Harry Elliott, et al.

RULING:
Defendant IMO Industries Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant IMO's motion for summary judgment and alternate motions are denied. Assuming, without deciding, that defendant shifted the burden of production on the issue of whether Mr. Elliott was exposed to products attributable to defendant, Mr. Elliott's declaration and deposition testimony create triable issues whether he was exposed to original gaskets and packing in defendant's pumps and turbines while Mr. Elliott worked for PG&E and whether the replacement packing and gaskets he was exposed to were supplied by defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Defendant failed to shift the burden of production on the question of whether any of its products contained asbestos because plaintiff was not asked about asbestos content at his deposition and defendant's interrogatories do not seek 'all facts.' Defendant failed to shift the burden of production on the issue of false representation because Plaintiff was never asked about representations made by defendant at either his deposition or by interrogatory. The Court overruled the Harry Elliott Declaration but did not rule on Charles Ay's declaration. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
